mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 60% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE crosses the 5.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand expansion that boosts corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over 5-year out-of-sample periods or fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark by 100 basis points annually.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of stable inflation reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain efficiency, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero with p greater than 0.10 during low inflation expansion regimes over a 4-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and consumer purchasing power, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The payroll growth signal produces a negative information coefficient over rolling 24-month windows or fails to predict return direction with accuracy above 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces central banks to tighten policy expectations, raising discount rates and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets record positive 20-day returns in over 65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with rising retail sales confirms broad-based economic expansion that lifts equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined GDP and retail sales signal yields a negative alpha of less than minus 0.5% per quarter over a 5-year out-of-sample evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of high interest rates signals supply-side contraction and rising input costs that compress profit margins and drive negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 58% of observations when industrial production falls during high rate regimes.
